top_categories.workout_context: 76 miles on paved Italian roads through Rome and the Castelli burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
86ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Campagnolo Roma's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
6,560ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Campagnolo Roma. Train it until it feels boring.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Granfondo Campagnolo Roma's paved Italian roads through Rome and the Castelli forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 76 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,560ft of climbing on paved Italian roads through Rome and the Castelli does to your power file on race day.
At Granfondo Campagnolo Roma, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Base fitness for Granfondo Campagnolo Roma. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Granfondo Campagnolo Roma, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved Italian roads through Rome and the Castelli at tempo for 76 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Campagnolo Roma’s 76 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 76 miles of paved italian roads through rome and the castelli romani volcanic hills in Rome, Lazio, Italy.
